questions.question: Does mediaPersistence.setBlob(groupId, plaintextSha256, data) store {bytes, type} such that a subsequent getBlob returns the same bytes and type per AC-21?
Does mediaPersistence.addMessageRef + clearGroupMedia result in getBlob returning null after clearGroupMedia is called per AC-22? Does clearGroupMedia also clear meta refs?
Is MarmotClient constructed in MarmotContext.tsx with a mediaFactory option using GroupMediaStore backed by the quizzl-media-blobs/blobs IDB store via IdbKeyValueStoreBackend per AC-23?
Does the leaveGroup function in MarmotContext.tsx call clearGroupMedia(groupId) between clearPollData and clearUnreadGroup per AC-24?
Are both 'quizzl-media-blobs' and 'quizzl-media-meta' added to the INDEXEDDB_DATABASES array in app/tests/e2e/helpers/clear-state.ts per AC-25?
Does the IDB key format use '{groupId}:{plaintextSha256}' for both blob and meta stores, ensuring group-scoped isolation (blobs for g1 don't affect g2)?
Does clearGroupMedia use a prefix filter ('{groupId}:') on the keys() list rather than a full clear(), so blobs from other groups are preserved?

Do the 12 unit tests cover: set/get round-trip, get returns null for missing key, group scoping, deleteBlob nullifies getBlob, addMessageRef round-trip, deduplication, removeMessageRef, clearGroupMedia removes blobs and meta but preserves other groups?
Does the test use the inline Map-backed idb-keyval mock pattern (consistent with pollPersistence.test.ts) rather than importing a mock library?

Is IdbKeyValueStoreBackend a separate adapter class (app/src/lib/marmot/idbKeyValueStoreBackend.ts) that bridges idb-keyval UseStore to KeyValueStoreBackend<T>, keeping mediaPersistence.ts and MarmotContext.tsx cleanly separated?

answers.evidence: setBlob at mediaPersistence.ts:13–19 stores {bytes, type} via set(blobKey(...), data). getBlob at lines 21–27 retrieves and returns same. Test mediaPersistence.test.ts:48–55 verifies round-trip with exact equality on bytes and type.

clearGroupMedia at mediaPersistence.ts:68–77 deletes keys from both blobStore and metaStore matching prefix. Test line 118–125 verifies blobs deleted, line 134–140 verifies meta refs cleared alongside blobs.
MarmotContext.tsx wires mediaFactory = () => new GroupMediaStore(new IdbKeyValueStoreBackend(mediaBlobStore)) into MarmotClient constructor. Uses quizzl-media-blobs IDB store via adapter.
MarmotContext.tsx leaveGroup function calls clearGroupMedia(groupId) between clearPollData and clearUnreadGroup, as verified by grep output.
clear-state.ts INDEXEDDB_DATABASES array includes both 'quizzl-media-blobs' and 'quizzl-media-meta', as verified by grep output.
blobKey() at mediaPersistence.ts:9–11 returns `${groupId}:${plaintextSha256}`. Test mediaPersistence.test.ts:62–66 verifies g1 and g2 blobs are isolated (g2 missing for same sha256).
clearGroupMedia at mediaPersistence.ts:68–77 uses prefix filter `k.startsWith(prefix)` then deletes individual keys. Preserves blobs from other groups. Test line 127–132 confirms.

12 tests cover: setBlob/getBlob round-trip (Q1, lines 47–66), deleteBlob (lines 69–76), addMessageRef/getMessageRefs round-trip + deduplication (lines 78–104), removeMessageRef (lines 106–115), clearGroupMedia removes all for groupId (lines 118–125), preserves other groups (lines 127–132), clears meta (lines 134–140).
Test mediaPersistence.test.ts uses inline Map-backed idb-keyval mock at lines 3–26 via vi.mock pattern. Consistent with pollPersistence.test.ts pattern (no external mock library).

IdbKeyValueStoreBackend is separate file at idbKeyValueStoreBackend.ts implementing getItem/setItem/removeItem/clear/keys. mediaPersistence.ts and MarmotContext.tsx import independently, clean separation.

codex_findings: Codex unavailable (disable-model-invocation). Manual verification: all 10 questions pass. No critical/high issues. Note: KeyValueStoreBackend is not in marmot-ts main index exports (deep subpath, type-only), so IdbKeyValueStoreBackend implements structurally without explicit implements clause—TypeScript structural typing ensures runtime compatibility.
